metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: full (exit_clean/sigkill/restart ported, commit d0e68929). Recorded as TWO variants (clean /quit + idle SIGKILL); the planned mid-turn-SIGKILL variant was DROPPED — the live recording proved kiro writes its transcript atomically at turn-end, so a running turn is never observably working and a mid-turn kill leaves an empty transcript with no session row.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: full (exit_clean / sigkill / restart ported from drive-codex-interactive.sh — commit d0e68929)

The session-end scenario is recorded as TWO variants on kiro-cli — (1) clean exit via /quit, (2) SIGKILL while idle in `ready`. A third variant (SIGKILL mid-turn while `working`) was in the original 3-variant plan but is NOT OBSERVABLE on kiro-cli and was dropped after the live recording proved why (see the recording-refutes note below). kiro-cli 2.5.1 supports both recorded teardowns as-shipped, the daemon observes the disappearance by design, and the driver now implements all three teardown step types.

## agent_supports: yes

Both recorded teardowns are native:

- **Variant 1 (clean exit).** `/quit` (alias `/exit`) ends the chat cleanly: kiro prints "Session ended.", removes the `<uuid>.lock` live-session marker, and the `kiro-cli` parent process exits. LIVE-verified in the recording: process_exited fires right after the session returns to ready.
- **Variant 2 (SIGKILL while idle).** `kill -9` on the `kiro-cli` parent PID (matched by cwd, mirroring DiscoverPIDByCWD) terminates the process while the session sits idle in `ready`; the daemon emits process_exited and no state ever advances to working afterward.

## daemon_capability: full

**Process-exit → session removed BY DESIGN.** The kiro-cli adapter matches on `ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}` and discovers the PID by cwd (`DiscoverPIDByCWD`). When the parent dies — clean `/quit` OR `kill -9` — the process scanner stops matching it, emits `process_exited`, and the row is removed. Both recorded variants show this live.

**The `ready → working → ready` arc** is produced by the parser: a `Prompt` event drives `ready → working`; the FINAL text-only `AssistantMessage` of a turn drives `working → ready`. Verified in the recording for both variants.

## RECORDING REFUTES THE 3rd VARIANT — kiro writes its transcript ATOMICALLY at turn-end

The original assessment planned a variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn while `working`), mirroring the claudecode session-end cell. The LIVE recording (events.jsonl 2026-06-05, commit below) proved this variant is UNOBSERVABLE on kiro-cli:

- kiro-cli does NOT stream its transcript. It buffers the ENTIRE turn (the `Prompt` line AND the `AssistantMessage`) in memory and FLUSHES the `<uuid>.jsonl` only when the turn COMPLETES. A live probe of a 500-word essay turn showed the transcript file at 0 bytes for the full 12 s the turn ran — nothing is written until the turn finishes.
- The daemon keys `working` off a non-empty transcript write (`transcript_activity`). With an empty file mid-turn, the session NEVER enters `working` while the turn is in flight; `working` materializes only at the instant of the turn-end flush, immediately followed by `ready` (the text-only AssistantMessage).
- Therefore a SIGKILL during a running turn kills kiro BEFORE any transcript line is written: the `<uuid>.jsonl` stays 0 bytes, the daemon never registers a session row, and there is NO process_exited for it. In the 3-variant recording, variant 3 left an empty `bdbca90d….jsonl` and produced ZERO events — no birth, no working, no exit.

Consequence: on a transcript-tailing daemon, kiro-cli cannot exhibit the "stuck in working after a crash" failure mode this scenario probes, because `working` exists only at the atomic turn-end flush, never durably mid-turn. The mid-turn-SIGKILL variant collapses into "no session was ever observed," which is not a meaningful session-end assertion. The cell is corrected to the two variants that ARE observable.

## driver_capability: full

The driver gap is closed: `exit_clean` (send /quit), `sigkill` (kill -9 the cwd-matched kiro-cli parent), and `restart` (fresh launch, new UUID, new cwd) are ported from drive-codex-interactive.sh and added to DRIVE_ELICITS. kiro mints its own UUID per launch and is discovered via a MARKER exactly like codex, so it adopts the shared _lib/drive slot model.

## Confidence

0.95 — every recorded phase is anchored in the LIVE events.jsonl; the dropped variant 3 is backed by a direct live probe of kiro's atomic-flush transcript behavior.
caveats: kiro-cli writes its transcript ATOMICALLY at turn-end (not streamed): mid-turn the <uuid>.jsonl is 0 bytes, so a session is never observably `working` while a turn runs. A SIGKILL mid-turn kills kiro before any line is written → empty transcript, no session row, no process_exited. The 3rd (mid-turn-SIGKILL) variant from the original plan was dropped for this reason; only clean-exit and idle-SIGKILL are recordable. Proven by a live probe (500-word essay turn = 0 transcript bytes for 12 s) and by variant 3 of the first recording (empty bdbca90d.jsonl, zero events).

sigkill targets the kiro-cli parent matched by cwd (pgrep -x kiro-cli + lsof cwd filter), mirroring the daemon's DiscoverPIDByCWD. pgrep -x never matches the always-running kiro_cli_desktop companion, and the cwd filter scopes the kill to the recording session only.

kiro-cli's JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields (FINDINGS.md §4); those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar which the parser does not read in v1. So observations assert model `unknown` and cost/tokens zero — orthogonal to this scenario, which asserts lifecycle only.

restart for kiro-cli launches a fresh kiro-cli in a NEW cwd (new UUID); kiro launches with persisted --trust-all-tools consent so a fresh cwd never stalls on a trust picker. A fresh cwd gives each variant an unambiguous cwd-based PID match across the variants.

sources.note: §8 /quit prints 'Session ended.' + removes <uuid>.lock + 'Resume with…'; §1 process tree (parent comm=kiro-cli, no kiro_cli_desktop collision); §2 live-append; §5 turn-end shape (text-only AssistantMessage = turn_done). LIVE-verified: /quit removed the session from the dashboard (process exit → session deleted by design).

Match: ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}; PIDForSession: DiscoverPID. When pgrep -x kiro-cli stops matching the dead parent, the scanner emits process_exited and the ready-TTL removes the row — the disappearance the scenario asserts.

Prompt→user_message (ready→working); text-only AssistantMessage→turn_done (working→ready); mid-turn AssistantMessage carries toolUse and stays working — produces the per-variant ready→working→ready arc.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" only; exit_clean / sigkill / restart arms are present but stubbed not_implemented → driver=gap. record ports them from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh.
Reference 3-variant recipe (exit_clean / restart / sigkill) and the variant-3 tail-window caveat this cell mirrors.

LIVE recording 2026-06-05: variants 1 (clean /quit) and 2 (idle SIGKILL) captured cleanly (2 UUID sessions, 2 process_exited, ready→working→ready arcs). The original variant 3 (mid-turn SIGKILL) produced an EMPTY transcript and ZERO events — proving kiro writes its transcript atomically at turn-end, so a running turn is never observably `working` and a mid-turn kill yields no session row. A live probe (500-word essay = 0 transcript bytes for 12 s) confirmed the atomic-flush behavior.

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; launch with `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` so the per-tool picker never stalls — FINDINGS.md §8)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell isolated mode or --attach against a daemon started with --record + grant-all)
no other kiro-cli session is alive in any of the recipe's cwds (cwd-based PID match)
setup: Driver launches a fresh `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` per variant under <staging>/cwd, cwd-2, cwd-3; kiro mints its own <uuid>.jsonl per launch (discovered as the newest session file with mtime > MARKER).

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains 2 distinct UUID session_ids (one per variant)
events.jsonl contains 2 process_exited events, one per UUID
variant 1: the first UUID session returns to ready right after its turn completes, immediately preceding its process_exited (proves 'State is ready at exit')
variant 2: the second UUID session ends in ready (idle) when sigkill fires; no state_transition to working appears after process_exited
the viewer state band shows two colored arcs separated by a grey gap where no session is alive between variants
